headline: 'Operation Pushkin': Rare books, Georgian con artists and the shadow of the Kremlin
description: For two years, Georgian criminals orchestrated the theft of rare editions of works by Alexander Pushkin from major European libraries. The trial of five of them begins in Paris on June 9. It could reveal whether Russia was involved.

'The SpaceX stock market debut reads more like science fiction than a realistic business plan'
description: Elon Musk is basing the market valuation of his aerospace company, which is set to go public on the Nasdaq on Friday, on the promise of dominating technologies and markets that do not yet exist. In the space sector, however, the line between achievement and disaster is razor-thin, explains Le Monde columnist Stéphane Lauer.

headline: Paris Mayor Grégoire confronts the dark side of his predecessor's legacy
description: Sexual violence in after-school programs has overshadowed the start of the new mayor's term, as he must bear the legacy of Anne Hidalgo, whom he served as first deputy.

headline: 'Disclosure Day': Steven Spielberg goes back to the future
description: After two more personal, retrospective feature films, the American filmmaker revisits his passion for extraterrestrials.

headline: 'Lyhanna was a victim twice': Women-led rallies across France condemn failures after schoolgirl's death
description: Several thousand people gathered on Monday in front of courthouses and city halls to express their dismay at the judicial treatment of sexual violence committed against children. Many demonstrators called for the resignation of Justice Minister Gérald Darmanin.

description: 'Resisting a repressive regime (3/3). Six years after the end of the pro-democracy movement that shook the country, judicial harassment continues to target those still seeking to promote freedom of expression.

headline: France revives Gratitude Train for the US's 250th anniversary
description: A restored railcar from northern France will retrace the journey of the Gratitude Train, which carried gifts from Le Havre to New York in 1949.

headline: 'I am a libertarian and belong to no party': The octogenarian, his anarchist friends and the mystery of a bomb at McDonald's
description: In July 2025, an explosive device and a torn Israeli flag were left in front of a McDonald's in France. After a 10-month investigation, an 85-year-old former press photographer, who claims to be outraged by the war in Gaza, emerged as the main suspect, while a possible accomplice died by suicide on May 7, just before an anti-terrorism operation.
